summary: Cache live-fetched surah metadata under a plan-private resource key (plan_surah_meta) so later offline plan commands read it locally without touching the shared 'surah' cache that sync owns.
reason: The generated fallback fetched /surahs to build a plan but never cached it, so a subsequent 'plan today'/'plan status' still saw <114 local rows, refetched, and failed offline. Caching into the shared 'surah' cache would risk downgrading richer synced rows, so a plan-private key is used instead.
validated_outcome: After 'plan start' (live), 'plan today --data-source local' returns the correct range (1:1 to 2:201) with no network call; the shared 'surah' resource is left untouched.
